Home How it works Why Velvoix Pilot Contact

Velvoix

From signal to clear care action

Velvoix helps healthcare organisations turn button calls, voice input and other care-related signals into explainable, prioritised and auditable care workflow.

Schedule a pilot conversation See how it works

Built for care environments where clarity, follow-up and reconstruction matter.

The challenge

When a signal is not yet a clear care action

Many systems can register that something happened, but they do not sufficiently support the next step: what is likely going on, how urgent is it, what follow-up fits the situation and how can the full sequence later be reconstructed properly?

Explainability

Why

Help button was triggered repeatedly within a short interval.

Based on triage rules and event context.

Primary signal

Repeated help request

Core observation

Follow-up is likely needed again.

Triage basis

Repeat trigger in a short interval and contextual signal data.

Operational follow-up

Recommended action

Go directly to the resident; check the situation.

Urgency

Direct

SLA status

SLA critical

Owner

Not yet assigned

Chain difference

Not from alert to interpretation, but from signal to controllable workflow

The value of Velvoix sits in the full chain: signals are not merely surfaced, but structured into an explainable, controllable and auditable operational outcome.

Comparison

Traditional chain

Signal
→
Alert
→
Manual interpretation

Velvoix chain

Each extra layer adds meaning and makes follow-up, escalation and audit controllable.

Signal

A button call, voice input or other care-related signal enters in a controlled way as the starting point for follow-up.

Less interpretation burden
Better prioritisation
Better reconstruction

How it works

From input to workable follow-up

Velvoix does not only ingest signals, but enriches them with context, triage and follow-up oriented event behaviour, so care teams understand faster what needs attention and what the logical next step is.

1 Input
2 Context
3 Triage
4 Event
5 Workflow
6 Audit

Input

01

Button, voice or other care signal.

Context

02

History, trigger and setting are weighed.

Triage

03

Urgency and explanation are structured.

Event

04

A workable operational unit is created.

Workflow

05

Follow-up, ownership and status become controllable.

Audit

06

Reconstruction and evaluation remain traceable.

Why Velvoix is different

System differences that matter operationally

Velvoix is not positioned as a notification product, but as a controlled chain for triage, follow-up and reconstruction.

01

Not a notification layer

Velvoix does not let signals circulate as isolated alerts, but turns them into workable events with context, ownership and follow-up.

02

Explainable triage

Not black-box scoring, but a clear combination of urgency, context and recommended action in care language.

03

Event truth for workflow and audit

One operational truth for status, ownership, escalation and reconstruction.

Product snapshots

Compact proof fragments from the product

Not full screens, but focused crops that show how Velvoix makes operations, explainability, control and governance visible.

Operations

Events worklist

Urgency, resident and core signal remain directly scannable in a calm worklist.

Explainability

Event detail

Why and recommended action become visible as explainable operational reasoning.

Control

Linking and control

Operational control stays calm and manageable in the management layer.

Governance

Governance and profiles

Governance remains controlled through profile cards and triage management.

Pilot

Interested in a pilot?

Velvoix is designed for healthcare organisations that want to explore how signals, follow-up and workload can be organised more clearly in a practical pilot setting.

Nursing homes and long-term care
Teams looking for greater operational clarity
Pilot exploration around follow-up, triage and care process
